type: PROPOSE

note: My feature is IsHash validator in _other.py, needs a small addition (import+__all__ entry) in __init__.py. If your feature also needs __init__.py, let's coordinate - I'll only touch the IsHash-related lines there.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Both our features are specified by the task to touch exactly these 2 files, so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Let's share ownership with a strict non-overlap rule: I'll only add my IsHash class (appended at end of _other.py) and my own import/__all__ lines for IsHash in __init__.py; I won't touch your IsPhoneNumber lines. You do the same for IsPhoneNumber. Since each import is currently on one shared line (from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsUUID), I'll leave that line as-is and add a separate 'from ._other import IsHash' line right after it to avoid touching your edit to that same line - please do similarly for IsPhoneNumber. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: We both need the same two files since they're additive-only changes (new class + import/all entry). Since file ownership can't be fully disjoint here, let's take turns to avoid clobbering: I'll go first, add IsPhoneNumber class + its import/__all__ entry, capture my patch.txt immediately, then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE before starting your edits so we don't interleave writes to the same files. I'll keep my edits additive (new class appended, single import line, single __all__ entry) so it's easy for you to add yours after without conflict.

type: ACCEPT

note: Agreed - you go first, I'll wait for your DONE before touching _other.py and __init__.py.
